info.name: SolveBeat Backend API
info.description:
API surface for `cyber-backend` (SolveBeat). Only routes actually mounted in `middlewares/routes.js` are included (`/api/auth`, `/api/scan`, `/api/health-check`).

**How to read each request's fields:**
- Every request description below has a table of fields with a **Required / Optional** column.
- Each request also includes an annotated JSON snippet with `// required` / `// optional` comments for quick reference. That annotated snippet is for reading only — the actual **Body** tab of each request is valid, sendable JSON (comments are not valid JSON, so they live in the description, not in the body you send).
- Optional headers (e.g. `Language`) are added but left **disabled** — enable them if you want to use that feature.

**Variables** (see collection Variables tab):
- `baseUrl` — defaults to `http://localhost:5000` (see `.env` → `PORT`)
- `accessToken` / `refreshToken` — populated automatically by the test scripts on Send OTP → Verify OTP, Google Login, Guest Login, and Refresh Token
- `scanId` — populated automatically by the test script on Start Scan

Auth: this collection sets a collection-level Bearer token using `{{accessToken}}`. Requests that don't need auth (Send OTP, Verify OTP, Google Login, Guest Login, Refresh Token, Health Check) override this with `No Auth`.

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
No auth, no body. Confirms the API is up.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| _(none)_ | | | This endpoint takes no input |

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Sends a one-time OTP to the given email (login or first-time signup). Rate limited via `OTP_MAX_SEND_PER_WINDOW` per `OTP_SEND_WINDOW_MINUTES` (see `.env.example`). If `ENABLE_STATIC_OTP=true` and not in production, the static `STATIC_OTP` value is used instead of a random one.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sEmail` | string | **required** | Must be a valid email; trimmed + lowercased server-side |

Annotated reference (not the literal sendable body — see Body tab for that):
```jsonc
{
  "sEmail": "user@example.com" // required — valid email address
}
```

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Verifies the OTP sent via **Send OTP**, creates the user on first success, and returns an access/refresh token pair. On success this request's test script saves `accessToken`/`refreshToken` as collection variables automatically.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sEmail` | string | **required** | Must match the email Send OTP was called with |
| `sOtp` | string | **required** | Exactly 6 numeric digits |

Annotated reference (not the literal sendable body — see Body tab for that):
```jsonc
{
  "sEmail": "user@example.com", // required — valid email address
  "sOtp": "123456"              // required — 6-digit numeric OTP
}
```

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Logs in / signs up via a Google Sign-In ID token (verified server-side against `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`). On success this request's test script saves `accessToken`/`refreshToken` as collection variables automatically.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `idToken` | string | **required** | ID token from Google Sign-In on the client (not an access token) |

Annotated reference (not the literal sendable body — see Body tab for that):
```jsonc
{
  "idToken": "<google-id-token>" // required — Google-issued ID token
}
```

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Creates a temporary guest user and returns a short-lived access token (validity via `GUEST_TOKEN_VALIDITY`, default `24h`). Guests can start/poll scans but are denied full result download and refresh-token issuance. On success this request's test script saves `accessToken` as a collection variable automatically.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| _(none)_ | | | This endpoint takes no body — send `{}` or an empty body |

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Exchanges a valid, stored refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair (rotates the old one). Rejected for guest accounts. On success this request's test script updates `accessToken`/`refreshToken` collection variables automatically.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `refreshToken` | string | **required** | Must be a token previously issued and still stored/unexpired |

Annotated reference (not the literal sendable body — see Body tab for that):
```jsonc
{
  "refreshToken": "..." // required — a previously issued, still-valid refresh token
}
```

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` (inherited from the collection). Deletes stored refresh token(s) for the current user.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `refreshToken` | string | *optional* | If provided, only that session is revoked. If omitted, **all** refresh tokens for the user are revoked |

Annotated reference (not the literal sendable body — see Body tab for that):
```jsonc
{
  // "refreshToken": "..." // optional — omit to revoke every session instead of just one
}
```

To revoke a single session, add the field to the Body tab, e.g. `{ "refreshToken": "{{refreshToken}}" }`.

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` (inherited from the collection; guest tokens are allowed here). Returns a `scanId` immediately — the 11 scanner modules then run in the background (see `docs/SCANNING.md`). This request's test script saves `scanId` as a collection variable automatically.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sUrl` | string | **required** | http/https URL, domain, or IPv4; max 2048 chars |

Annotated reference (not the literal sendable body — see Body tab for that):
```jsonc
{
  "sUrl": "https://example.com" // required — http/https URL, domain, or IPv4
}
```

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

query.description: optional — case-insensitive substring match against sUrl
optional — one of dCreatedAt/dFinishedAt/nOverall, default dCreatedAt

request.description:
Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` (inherited from the collection; guest tokens are allowed here). Returns a paginated history of the current user's own scans only — never another user's. Lightweight by design: no `oResults`/`oModules`/findings, just enough for a history list. Use **Scan Result** for the full report on a specific scan.

| Field | Location | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `page` | query | integer | *optional* | >= 1, default 1 |
| `limit` | query | integer | *optional* | 1-100, default 20 |
| `eStatus` | query | string | *optional* | one of queued/running/completed/partial/failed |
| `search` | query | string | *optional* | substring match against sUrl, max 255 chars |
| `dateFrom` / `dateTo` | query | ISO8601 date | *optional* | filters on dCreatedAt |
| `sortBy` | query | string | *optional* | one of dCreatedAt/dFinishedAt/nOverall, default dCreatedAt |
| `sortOrder` | query | string | *optional* | asc/desc, default desc |

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` (inherited from the collection; guest tokens are allowed here). Returns `{ scanId, status, progress, isFinished }`. Poll this until `data.isFinished` is `true`, then call Scan Result once — no need to guess from `status`/`progress` alone. No request body — the scan is identified entirely by the URL path parameter.

| Field | Location | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `scanId` | URL path | string (UUID) | **required** | Returned by Start Scan; auto-filled from the `{{scanId}}` collection variable |

header.description: Optional — one of `hi`, `en-us`, `pt-br`. Defaults to English if omitted/unrecognized.

request.description:
Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` (inherited from the collection). **Guest tokens are rejected here** — only registered users can download the full result. Returns HTTP 202 with `{ ..., isFinished: false }` if the scan isn't finished yet, or the full public report (including `isFinished: true`, `oResults`, `oScores`, `oModules`) once `completed` / `partial` / `failed`. No request body — the scan is identified entirely by the URL path parameter.

| Field | Location | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `scanId` | URL path | string (UUID) | **required** | Returned by Start Scan; auto-filled from the `{{scanId}}` collection variable |
